name: Fatima bint Hussain
title: Brave Narrator
shortDescription: The learned and eloquent daughter of Imam Hussain (AS) who was present at Karbala and revealed the truths with her sermons in Kufa.
fullStory:
Fatima bint Hussain (AS) is the learned, eloquent, and brave daughter of Imam Hussain (AS) and Umm Ishaq. She bore a striking resemblance to her grandmother, Hazrat Fatima Zahra (SA). Fatima was present in the Master of Martyrs' caravan and witnessed all the bitter and painful events of Ashura closely.

After her father's martyrdom and the looting of the tents, Fatima was taken captive along with the other women and children. On the journey of captivity, she was always a protector of the children and, despite her young age (around 20 years old), stood like a firm mountain alongside her aunt, Lady Zaynab (SA).

One of Fatima bint Hussain's most important roles was delivering a crushing sermon in the market of Kufa. In this sermon, with clear and eloquent words, she rebuked the people of Kufa for their betrayal, saying: 'O people of Kufa! Woe unto you! What sin had we committed that you deemed our blood permissible?' Her sermon was so impactful that the sound of crying rose from the people.

Fatima bint Hussain is also a narrator of many Hadiths and events of Karbala, having narrated many traditions from her father and aunt. After returning to Medina, she spent years in worship and enlightenment, and finally passed away in Medina.
quoteArabic: يا أَهْلَ الكُوفَةِ! يا أَهْلَ المَكْرِ وَالغَدْرِ وَالخُيَلاءِ...
quote: (In the sermon of Kufa:) O people of Kufa! O people of deceit, treachery, and arrogance...
